text: Moving through these dim, echoing hallways, I feel as if I am crossing a threshold into a space both intellectual and experimental. The glass and steel convey a clinical detachment, while the subdued lighting intensifies my sense of solitude and anticipation. Within the R+D space, the vivid colors and thoughtful displays beckon my curiosity, yet I remain somewhat of an outsider, aware of my own observation and contemplation. There is a quiet thrill in this environment—a space of minds and machines mingling—where I am both participant and silent observer, awaiting meaning to unfold.
